steps.title: Map the two parts
steps.content: The passage first names two projects that use metal as examples of a contemporary Japanese design trend. It then explains the principle behind that trend: sleek modern metal accents coexist with traditional organic materials, reflecting technology and preservation.

steps.title: Reject distorted structures
steps.content: Choice A says the passage details events behind the buildings, which it does not. Choice B says it explains past uses of materials. Choice C says one approach has had more impact than the other, but the passage presents a combination. The structure moves from examples to underlying principles. The answer is D.
